title: Do not use registries if user specifies transport

comments.body: @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat @mtrmac @umohnani8 This is a partial solution but it at least fixes the case where user specifies a transport.  Don't use registries.  We need something similar to not use them if the user specifies a host/registryname.

@TomSweeneyRedHat @mtrmac @nalind @umohnani8 @pixdrift PTAL
I just want to get this in for today, and we need to really clean up  the handling of users specifying registry and/or transport.

> I just want to get this in for today

When faced with non-trivial code with unexpected behaviors in corner cases, my personal preference would be to stop adding more non-trivial code with unexpected corner cases, even when in a hurry :)

> This is a partial solution but it at least fixes the case where user specifies a transport.

I’m not 100% sure what ”the case” being fixed here is; is it all just better error messages, or is there something that just fails and shouldn’t have?

If you want to get _some_ useful error messages, just drop all the heuristics in `pullImage` to be
```go
	if err = cp.Image(ctx, policyContext, destRef, srcRef, getCopyOptions(options.ReportWriter, sc, nil, "")); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return destRef, nil
 
```
and, I don’t know, have `resolveImage` record the first `pullAndFindImage` error cause and return it at the end of the loop.

Or is it about 
https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/1212#issuecomment-410350051 ? I haven’t actually _tested_ that but it seems to me that the solution is also to drop the heuristics at the end of `pullImage` (the failure happens because `hasRegistry` at the end of `pullImage` chokes on `docker://…`).

---

BTW, a lot of this `buildah` code and the code in `podman` has obviously shared origins, even if they drifted away over time (and a large part of that drift is my fault). Is there any long-term idea that the two would eventually share code, and if so, in what direction?

E.g. the `buildah` `localImageNameForReference` / `podman` `pullGoalFromImageReference` set of heuristics should very likely be shared.
